scope: Final synthesis of the SENEA diligence stack. Consolidates claims-source-pack.json, financial-rebuild.json, industry-bear-case.json, and attribution-report.json into a claim-by-claim scorecard, financial / industry / attribution summaries, a mechanism-fit analog screen, a final verdict, and an enumerated unresolved-diligence list. Conclusions that lack anchored primary-source evidence are classified as 'unresolved' rather than asserted. This is a research artifact, not investment advice.
researchDisclaimer: This is a research report. It is NOT investment advice, NOT a recommendation, and NOT a solicitation to buy or sell any security. Conclusions classified as 'unresolved' or 'partial' are not assertions of fact; they require the primary-source pulls enumerated in unresolvedDiligenceItems before any decision-relevant use.

claimScorecard.claimText: Seneca trades below tangible book value on a FIFO-adjusted basis.
claimScorecard.anchoredEvidence: FY2025 LIFO reserve $359.3m anchored as the FIFO add-back input (S-001).

claimScorecard.claimText: Seneca trades at or below NCAV when inventory is restated to FIFO.
claimScorecard.anchoredEvidence: FY2025 LIFO reserve $359.3m anchored as the FIFO add-back input (S-001).

claimScorecard.claimText: Seneca's reported earnings materially understate FIFO earnings power because of a $359.3m LIFO reserve.
claimScorecard.anchoredEvidence: FY2025 LIFO reserve $359.3m anchored verbatim from FY2025 10-K (S-001).

claimScorecard.claimText: Seneca has compounded book value per share over a 20-year window despite minimal sell-side coverage.
claimScorecard.anchoredEvidence: Bridge specified; endpoints not yet retrieved.

claimScorecard.claimText: U.S. private-label vegetable canning is an oligopoly with Seneca and Lakeside Foods accounting for ~90% of supply.
claimScorecard.anchoredEvidence: Lakeside corporate statement at the pointer level; not triangulated against syndicated panel data.

claimScorecard.claimText: Del Monte Pacific deconsolidated its U.S. Del Monte Foods business effective May 2025; the U.S. subsidiary subsequently filed for Chapter 11.
claimScorecard.anchoredEvidence: Both deconsolidation and Chapter 11 are pointer-anchored in primary filings and the PACER docket.

claimScorecard.claimText: SENEA was deleted from the S&P SmallCap 600 in July 2023, producing forced selling pressure independent of fundamentals.
claimScorecard.anchoredEvidence: Deletion announced by S&P DJI; rebalance mechanism documented (IJR / SLY).

claimScorecard.claimText: Seneca has executed material share repurchases relative to float, supporting per-share compounding.
claimScorecard.anchoredEvidence: Sign positive at the directional level; magnitude requires Item 5 / DEF 14A retrieval.

claimScorecard.claimText: Seneca has minimal sell-side analyst coverage, contributing to the discount.
claimScorecard.anchoredEvidence: Pointer-anchored via Seneca IR coverage page.

claimScorecard.claimText: Seneca operates a dual-class share structure with concentrated insider/family voting control.
claimScorecard.anchoredEvidence: Dual-class structure pointer-anchored; combined family / insider voting share pending P-009.

claimScorecard.claimText: Seneca has demonstrated pricing-cost recovery across crop, steel/tinplate, freight, and labor cycles.
claimScorecard.anchoredEvidence: Pass-through hypothesis stated in plan; bridge methodology defined; not computed.

tangibleBookBridge.method: stockholders_equity - goodwill - intangibles + (lifo_reserve * (1 - effective_tax_rate)), divided by Class A + Class B shares.
tangibleBookBridge.fifoAddBackAfterTaxNote: Derived as 359.3 * (1 - 0.21). State-inclusive effective rates compress this further; bear case B-010.

ncavBridge.method: total_current_assets - total_liabilities + (lifo_reserve * (1 - effective_tax_rate)), divided by Class A + Class B shares.

earningsPowerBridge.method: fifo_pretax_income = reported_pretax_income + (lifo_reserve_FY2025 - lifo_reserve_FY2024); fifo_net_earnings = reported_net_earnings + lifo_charge * (1 - effective_tax_rate); fifo_ebitda = reported_ebitda + lifo_charge.

perShareCompoundingBridge.method: Reconstruct per-share book value FY2005 to FY2025 from historical 10-Ks; compute LIFO-basis and FIFO-adjusted CAGRs.

financialSummary.summaryConclusion: FIFO earnings power exists at >$0 (anchored at the $359.3m LIFO reserve). Whether SENEA is below FIFO tangible book or FIFO NCAV at the 2026-04-30 reference price is unresolved because reported equity, goodwill, intangibles, current assets, total liabilities, share count, and a price snapshot have not been retrieved.

bindingBearItems.title: Del Monte may emerge leaner from Chapter 11
Tinplate / steel cost inflation outruns price recovery
After-tax LIFO add-back is smaller than gross

industrySummary.summaryConclusion: Bull industry-structure narrative (Seneca + Lakeside duopoly with Del Monte exiting) is directionally credible at the source-pointer level but is graded 'unverified' or 'partially-verified' until P-001 through P-009 close. Bear case is high-conviction-qualitative; eight of ten items bind even under the strongest verified bull narrative.

masterDataDependency.title: SENEA Class A daily OHLCV from a consolidated tape vendor
masterDataDependency.rationale: All eleven event-window magnitudes are blocked on S-018; inventing returns in its absence would violate the upstream acceptance criterion.

attributionSummary.summaryConclusion: The SENEA discount is best modeled as the sum of (a) a one-time index-deletion supply shock (E-001), (b) structural liquidity / coverage / governance discounts (E-008, E-009, E-011), (c) a beta-driven liquidity-and-factor drag through 2022-2024 with partial 2025 recovery (E-007, E-010), and (d) two competitor-restructuring positives that have not yet repriced (E-004, E-005). Fundamental repricing on FIFO earnings power (E-002, E-003) and the commodity / cost-recovery bridge (E-006) are methodologically defined but quantitatively pending.

analogCandidates.mechanismFitNote: Closest industry analog: private-label canned vegetables / sauces, family-controlled dual-class, very thin float, LIFO accounting. Confirms the SENEA mechanism rather than diversifies it.
Dual-class family-controlled grocer with LIFO, owned-real-estate book, low coverage, multi-decade buyback history. Same mechanism class as SENEA across food retail.

Family-controlled dual-class department store with LIFO, owned-real-estate base, structural buybacks of historic magnitude. Coverage exists but mechanism is extreme; included to size the M2/M5/M6 confluence.
Family-controlled grocer with LIFO, owned-real-estate book, undercovered. Buyback program smaller than DDS/IMKTA.
Family-controlled (Class A super-voting) snack / nut processor; LIFO inventories; modest sell-side coverage; durable private-label-heavy customer base.
Canadian family-controlled dual-class fruit-juice / private-label processor; asset-backed; under-covered ex-Canada. Mechanism analog without the U.S. LIFO tax effect.
Family-controlled snack / frozen-dough manufacturer; LIFO; very thinly traded; small-cap-index-vulnerable.

Founding-family dual-class (super-voting) confectionery; durable niche; conservative balance sheet; persistent buybacks. Tends to trade at a premium not a discount; included as a mechanism reference, not a value comparable.
Founding-family dual-class egg producer; asset-backed agricultural niche; mechanism partial (no LIFO inventory cushion of canning scale; dividend-led capital return rather than buyback-led).
Family-controlled stainless-steel processing-equipment manufacturer; LIFO; very thin float; SEC-reporting but dark-stock-like. Strong M1/M2/M3/M4/M5 overlap with SENEA.
Steel-coil processor; LIFO; small-cap-index-vulnerable; asset-backed. Insider ownership material but not strict family-control.
Steel service center with LIFO; insider ownership material; durable mid-stream niche; periodic buyback.

analogScreenMethodology.scoringConvention: Y = mechanism present at the descriptive level; ~ = partial / smaller in magnitude; N = mechanism not present. All scores are descriptive priors and require primary-source verification per ticker.
analogScreenMethodology.verificationProtocol: Mirror SENEA workflow per ticker: 10-K balance sheet for M1/M2, proxy for M5, IR coverage page for M3, ETF/index membership for M4, Item 5 buyback table for M6, Item 1 'Business' for M7.

finalVerdict.summary: The SENEA opportunity is mechanism-rich and evidence-incomplete. The bull thesis is structurally coherent but quantitatively unproven in this diligence pass: the only numerically anchored data point is the FY2025 LIFO reserve of $359.3m (S-001, claim C-003). Of eleven upstream claims, zero are verified, five are partial (C-003, C-006, C-007, C-009, C-010), and six are unresolved (C-001, C-002, C-004, C-005, C-008, C-011). No claim has been refuted. The bear case binds at multiple points (B-001, B-002, B-003, B-005, B-007, B-010) regardless of how the bull narrative resolves.
finalVerdict.priceTargetReason: A price target is intentionally not produced. The two valuation bridges that would produce one (tangibleBookBridge and ncavBridge in financial-rebuild.json) are blocked on F-002 (effective tax rate) and F-003 (balance-sheet line items). Inventing a number would violate the 'classify as unresolved if evidence is missing' acceptance criterion.

biggestUnblockingDependency.title: Seneca FY2025 10-K (LIFO note, Income Taxes note, balance sheet, Item 5 buybacks)

secondaryUnblockingDependencies.title: SENEA Class A reference price snapshot

finalVerdict.decisionRecommendation: Do not size a position from this artifact alone. Run the closing pulls (S-001, S-005, S-009, S-018) and rebuild the four valuation bridges before any decision-relevant use. Use the analog screen as a watchlist for the same mechanism profile, not as an alternative to verifying SENEA itself.
finalVerdict.researchDisclaimer: This is a research report. Not investment advice.

unresolvedDiligenceItems.description: Identify the specific competitor named alongside Lakeside in the ~90% private-label statement.
Determine whether the FY2025 10-K discloses a maintenance-capex figure or proxy.
Confirm precise effective date of the July 2023 S&P SmallCap 600 deletion and identify rebalancing index ETFs.
Confirm Del Monte US Chapter 11 filing entity (Inc. vs Holdings) and posture (reorganization vs 363 vs liquidation).
Determine whether management's FIFO-adjusted earnings / EBITDA are reconciled in the FY2025 10-K itself or only in supplemental investor materials.
Quantify combined Class A + Class B voting power held by founding family / insiders per FY2025 DEF 14A.
Retrieve FY2024 LIFO reserve to compute the FY2025 LIFO charge.
Retrieve FY2025 effective tax rate from the Income Taxes note.
Retrieve FY2025 consolidated balance sheet (equity, goodwill, intangibles, current assets, total liabilities, debt, cash).
Retrieve FY2025 Item 5 monthly repurchase table and FY2025 DEF 14A buyback authorization disclosures.
Retrieve FY2005-FY2024 10-K filings for the 20-year per-share compounding rebuild.
Capture an archived SENEA Class A closing price at or near 2026-04-30.
Confirm cover-page share counts on the FY2025 10-K and most recent 10-Q.
Confirm whether maintenance-capex disclosure exists or must be PP&E-roll-forward-derived.
Quote Item 1 of Seneca FY2025 10-K on competitive position.
Archive Lakeside ~90% statement verbatim with date and venue; triangulate against Circana / NielsenIQ and Census ASM.
Catalog Hanover, Faribault, Furmano's, Truitt, Allens / Sager Creek with corporate-website and trade-press references.
Pull Del Monte US Chapter 11 PACER docket; confirm filing entity, filing date, and posture.
Quote Del Monte Pacific FY2025 annual report deconsolidation paragraphs.
FY2018-FY2025 revenue / gross-margin bridge against tinplate / steel / freight indices.
Retrieve and quote USDA ERS, Census ASM, and Circana / NielsenIQ canned-vegetable category trend.
Quote Item 1 / Item 1A of Seneca FY2025 10-K on customer concentration.
Confirm combined Class A / Class B voting power per FY2025 DEF 14A.
Effective deletion date for E-001 (carry-forward of Q-003).
Per-release reported financials and FY2024 LIFO reserve (carry-forward of F-001, F-003).
FY2025 effective tax rate (carry-forward of F-002).
Buyback authorization and execution dates (carry-forward of F-004).
Del Monte US Chapter 11 filing entity and posture (carry-forward of Q-004).
Quoted Del Monte Pacific deconsolidation paragraphs (carry-forward of P-005).
Combined family / insider voting power (carry-forward of Q-006, P-009).
FY2018-FY2025 revenue / gross-margin bridge (carry-forward of P-006).
S-018 (SENEA Class A daily OHLCV) is the master event-window dependency.
Joint Fama-French regression to avoid double-counting between E-007 and E-010.
13F-aggregate ownership concentration pre-/post-July-2023 to separate E-001 from E-011.

Per-ticker primary-source verification for every analog candidate in analog-screen.csv. Mirror SENEA workflow: 10-K balance sheet, proxy, IR coverage, ETF/index membership, Item 5 buyback table, Item 1 'Business'.
A SENEA price target is intentionally not produced. Compute downstream once F-002 and F-003 close, using tangibleBookBridge and ncavBridge from financial-rebuild.json.
Formal Fama-French / liquidity-factor variance decomposition of the SENEA discount intentionally not produced because S-018, S-019, S-021 not retrieved.
